retrieved.title: Additional exams
retrieved.text: For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. retrieved.text: / - I've already passed the additional exam in Latin/Greek, but it still shows up in my student record sheet. What am I supposed to do? The completion of the additional exam in Latin or Greek should be registered automatically. If your school reports prove that you have sufficient knowledge, this is done directly during the first-time admission procedure. If you did not present the appropriate school reports at the Admission Office, you can send them via [contact form](/en/contact-form/). Please include your u:account e-mail address in your message. In case you take the additional exam at the University of Vienna, the [StudienServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at) of the corresponding course will register your grade. If this does not happen, please contact them directly. - I'm already studying at the University of Vienna. May I pass the additional exam which is required for a different degree programme at the University of Vienna? You are already studying at the University of Vienna and would like to start a second degree programme, for which Latin/Greek is required? In this case, you can pass the additional exam in Latin/Greek at the University of Vienna if you have already completed the STEOP of your current degree programme. retrieved.title: Additional exams
retrieved.text:
For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Greek has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Greek at a secondary school after the 8th grade for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission.

- Prior to admission:
  For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Greek at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) about your options.
- During the degree programme:
  For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Greek during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes).

If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet.

The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology. You get all information at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/studium/zusatz-und-ergaenzungspruefungen/).

retrieved.text: "subject-specific" supplementary examinations can comprise several courses and must be taken additionally. They are best taken at the beginning of the master degree programme. Example: Laura has completed her bachelor degree in Astronomy and would now like to study for the master programme in Meteorology. She has received a letter of admission in which the following supplementary examinations are prescribed:VU Dynamik I (6 ECTS), VO Dynamik II (4 ECTS) and VU Klimasystem der Erde (8 ECTS) totalling 18 ECTS. She can be admitted to the master degree programme in Meteorology, but after admission she must successfully complete the courses "VU Dynamik I", "VO Dynamik II" and "VU Klimasystem der Erde" by the end of the second semester in addition to the coursework prescribed in the degree programme. - How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have already completed examinations in the non-degree programme and supplementary examinations to the extent of 30 ECTS or less remain? Apply for admission to the desired [master programme](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) in u:space within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- Which supplementary examinations do I have to make up for?

  You can find the required supplementary examinations in your rejection letter. If you have any questions, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) in which the course if offered.

retrieved.title: Additional degree programmes
retrieved.text:
You are currently admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and would like to study an additional degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-master-programme/)

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

reranked.text:
For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Greek has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Greek at a secondary school after the 8th grade for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission.

- Prior to admission:
  For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Greek at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) about your options.
- During the degree programme:
  For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Greek during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes).

If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet.

The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology. You get all information at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/studium/zusatz-und-ergaenzungspruefungen/).

  You have already completed examinations in the non-degree programme and supplementary examinations to the extent of 30 ECTS or less remain? Apply for admission to the desired [master programme](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) in u:space within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- Which supplementary examinations do I have to make up for?

  You can find the required supplementary examinations in your rejection letter. If you have any questions, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) in which the course if offered.

reranked.title: Additional degree programmes
reranked.text:
You are currently admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and would like to study an additional degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-master-programme/)

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

selected.text: For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Additional exams — Latin
For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin.
